node-anatomy: CONTENT: Anatomy of ONE Ray node. A single big dashed muted-teal rounded container labeled "Node" (dashed border only, no black outer box). Inside, top: a cool-grey box "Raylet" containing two sub-boxes: "Scheduler" (cool-grey) and a muted-teal box "Object Store (Plasma)". Below: a steel-blue box "Worker", and inside/attached to it two small boxes: "Ownership Table" (pale blue-grey) and "In-process Store" (pale blue-grey). To the side, an ochre-red box "GCS (head only)" and a sage-green box "Driver". One ochre-red caption points at Raylet: "one Raylet per node: schedules + stores big objects".
memory: CONTENT: Four kinds of memory in Ray, shown as four labeled rounded boxes in a row, each a different flat color: 1) steel-blue "Task/Actor Heap" (running user code), 2) muted-teal "Shared Object Store" (big objects, cross-node), 3) pale blue-grey "In-process Store" (small objects < 100KB), 4) cool-grey "System Metadata" (~KB per ObjectRef). Under each box a one-line English caption of what it holds. One ochre-red caption at the bottom: "big objects -> shared store; small objects -> in-process".
cluster: CONTENT: Ray cluster overview. Three dashed muted-teal rounded Node containers side by side labeled "Node 1", "Node 2", "Node 3" (dashed borders only, no black outer box), each containing a cool-grey "Scheduler", a steel-blue "Worker", and a muted-teal "Object Store". One of them (Node 1, the head node) additionally has an ochre-red box "GCS" and a sage-green "Driver" on top. Thin ink-black double-arrows connect the three Schedulers (distributed scheduling) and connect the three Object Stores (object transfer). One ochre-red caption points at GCS: "GCS lives only on the head node".
concepts: CONTENT: The five core Ray concepts as five labeled rounded boxes with a one-line English definition under each: steel-blue "Task" (a remote function call, returns an ObjectRef), muted-teal "Object" (immutable value, referenced by ObjectRef), steel-blue "Actor" (stateful worker), sage-green "Driver" (program entry, submits tasks), pale blue-grey "Job" (all tasks/objects of one Driver). Arrange in a clean row or 2x3 grid. One ochre-red caption: ".remote() returns an ObjectRef immediately, not the result".
